title: Book Of Nonsense Limerick 64.
content:
There was an Old Person of Troy,
    Whose drink was warm brandy and soy;
    Which he took with a spoon,
    By the light of the moon,
    In sight of the city of Troy.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Edward Lear, titled "Book Of Nonsense Limerick 64.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
